dataset: Fiji built-in Fly Brain confocal sample

pi_items.source: Confirmed via Fiji/ImageJ sample image repository documentation and ImageJ Prefs.getImagesURL() method which provides access to 'flybrain.zip'

pi_items.verified_value: Fiji/ImageJ built-in sample from imagej.nih.gov sample repository
pi_items.source: Confirmed via ImageJ documentation showing flybrain is accessible via File > Open Samples and Prefs.getImagesURL() + 'flybrain.zip'. Sample was contributed by Benjamin Schmid, author of the ImageJ 3D Viewer, and is from the Heisenberg lab in Wuerzburg, Germany

Could not find authoritative documentation specifying exact dimensions. However, consistent with VIB Protocol documentation mentioning downsampling 1024x1024x256 to 256x256x64 (similar to claimed 256x256x57). The sample description states 'original image was smoothed and resampled 4 times in each direction', consistent with 4x linear downsampling. 256x256 is standard for confocal microscopy, and 57 z-slices is reasonable for a Drosophila brain stack

privileged_info_summary: The Fly Brain sample is a real confocal microscopy image of an adult Drosophila brain from the ImageJ/Fiji built-in sample collection. It is a legitimate scientific image (not synthetic) from the Heisenberg lab in Wuerzburg, Germany, showing nc82 antibody staining (green) and GAL4 driver line 78y expression (red). The image was contributed by Benjamin Schmid, author of the ImageJ 3D Viewer. It is accessed via ImageJ's Prefs.getImagesURL() method and File > Open Samples menu. The exact dimensions [256, 256, 57] could not be independently verified from authoritative sources, though they are consistent with the documented 4x resampling of the original confocal stack.
